verses.text: He that is often reproved yet hardens his neck 'shall suddenly be destroyed, and that without remedy'; when the righteous rule, the people rejoice; he that loves wisdom gladdens his father, but the companion of harlots wastes wealth. Refused reproof brings ruin. Righteous rule and wise sons bring joy.

He that is often reproved yet hardens his neck 'shall suddenly be destroyed, and that without remedy'; when the righteous rule, the people rejoice; he that loves wisdom gladdens his father, but the companion of harlots wastes wealth. Refused reproof brings ruin. Righteous rule and wise sons bring joy.

He that is often reproved yet hardens his neck 'shall suddenly be destroyed, and that without remedy'; when the righteous rule, the people rejoice; he that loves wisdom gladdens his father, but the companion of harlots wastes wealth. Refused reproof brings ruin. Righteous rule and wise sons bring joy.

A just king establishes the land, but a taker of bribes overthrows it; a flatterer spreads a net for his neighbour's feet; the wicked is snared by his own sin, but the righteous sing and rejoice. Justice steadies a nation. Flattery and sin trap their own.

A just king establishes the land, but a taker of bribes overthrows it; a flatterer spreads a net for his neighbour's feet; the wicked is snared by his own sin, but the righteous sing and rejoice. Justice steadies a nation. Flattery and sin trap their own.

A just king establishes the land, but a taker of bribes overthrows it; a flatterer spreads a net for his neighbour's feet; the wicked is snared by his own sin, but the righteous sing and rejoice. Justice steadies a nation. Flattery and sin trap their own.

The righteous considers the cause of the poor, which the wicked ignores; scornful men inflame a city, but the wise turn away wrath; with a fool there is no rest, only raging and laughter. The godly heed the poor. The wise quiet strife where fools only stir it.

The righteous considers the cause of the poor, which the wicked ignores; scornful men inflame a city, but the wise turn away wrath; with a fool there is no rest, only raging and laughter. The godly heed the poor. The wise quiet strife where fools only stir it.

The righteous considers the cause of the poor, which the wicked ignores; scornful men inflame a city, but the wise turn away wrath; with a fool there is no rest, only raging and laughter. The godly heed the poor. The wise quiet strife where fools only stir it.

The bloodthirsty hate the upright, but the just seek his soul; 'a fool uttereth all his mind: but a wise man keepeth it in'; if a ruler hearkens to lies, all his servants are wicked. The wise restrain their words. A leader who loves falsehood corrupts his whole house.

The bloodthirsty hate the upright, but the just seek his soul; 'a fool uttereth all his mind: but a wise man keepeth it in'; if a ruler hearkens to lies, all his servants are wicked. The wise restrain their words. A leader who loves falsehood corrupts his whole house.

The poor and the deceitful meet, and the LORD lightens both their eyes; a king who judges the poor faithfully has a lasting throne; 'the rod and reproof give wisdom: but a child left to himself bringeth his mother to shame.' God made rich and poor. Correction shapes a child for good.

When the wicked multiply, so does sin, but the righteous shall see their fall; correct your son, and he shall give rest and delight; 'where there is no vision, the people perish: but he that keepeth the law, happy is he.' God's word steadies a people. Without revelation, a nation runs wild.

A servant is not corrected by words alone, for he understands yet will not answer; 'seest thou a man that is hasty in his words? there is more hope of a fool than of him'; a pampered servant becomes a son in the end. Hasty speech is dangerous. Discipline, not indulgence, forms character.

An angry man stirs strife, and a furious man abounds in transgression; a man's pride brings him low, but the humble in spirit gains honour; whoso shares with a thief hates his own soul. Anger and pride destroy. Humility is the path to honour.

An angry man stirs strife, and a furious man abounds in transgression; a man's pride brings him low, but the humble in spirit gains honour; whoso shares with a thief hates his own soul. Anger and pride destroy. Humility is the path to honour.

'The fear of man bringeth a snare: but whoso putteth his trust in the LORD shall be safe'; many seek the ruler's favour, but every man's judgment comes from the LORD; the unjust and the upright are each an abomination to the other. Fear God, not man. True justice is His to give.
